# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Python data processing application. Start from a full Ubuntu base image and install Python, pip, curl, wget, git, vim, and other useful development tools. This gives us flexibility for debugging and running various scripts in production.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:latest

# Update the package list
RUN apt update && apt -y upgrade

# Install development tools
RUN apt-get install -y \
    build-essential \
    git \
    vim \
    curl \
    wget \
    unzip \
    libssl-dev \
    zlib1g-dev \
    python3-dev \
    libxml2-dev \
    libxslt1-dev

# Install Python and pip
RUN apt-get install -y python3-pip && ln -s /usr/bin/python3 /usr/local/bin/python

# Set environment variables
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND noninteractive

# Install required packages via pip
RUN pip3 install --upgrade pip && \
    pip3 install pandas numpy scipy matplotlib

# Make the current directory as the working directory in the container
WORKDIR /app

# Copy requirements file to the container and install required packages
COPY requirements.txt .

# Run the command to install dependencies
RUN pip3 install -r requirements.txt

# Make port 80 available to the world outside this container
EXPOSE 80

# Define environment variable
ENV NAME World
